title: US5: AI triage application (demo) — auto-trigger on ticket.created + Mode-1 triage + gated draft_reply → approve → reply
description: Spec 003 US5 (the acceptance demo). Builds on l29's async event-driven run trigger. An enabled agent auto-runs on ticket.created (loop-guarded: subscribe ONLY to ticket.created, never message.received, so an agent reply can't re-trigger). Mode 1 auto-applies reversible triage (status/priority/tags/assignee) and queues the External draft_reply; a human approves in the US4 queue and the reply is sent via the existing ticketing.Reply outbox. Architecture: (1) ticket.created subscriber creates a queued agent_run per enabled agent, idempotent on the triggering message_id; (2) a run-drain poller claims queued runs (queued->running state-claim, SKIP LOCKED, system-wide definer fn) and calls Engine.Execute. Reuses the US4 approve->outbox->idempotent-executor pattern symmetrically. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-03-us5-triage-demo.md

title: US3: Agent run loop + internal tool registry + audit + cost + budget guard
description:
Spec 003 US3 (design §5). Build the bounded agentic run loop that drives an agent definition (US2) through the AI gateway (US1) to act on spec-002 tickets.

Scope:
- agent_run table (RLS, migration 0028; design §3.2): id, agent_id, business_id, tenant_root_id, trigger, target_type/target_id, status(queued/running/awaiting_approval/succeeded/failed), tokens_in/out, cost_cents, correlation_id, error, timestamps.
- Internal tool registry wrapping ticketing.Service (read_ticket, read_thread, set_status, set_priority, set_tags, set_assignee, draft_reply) — each carries a JSON-schema + static effect-class (Safe/External/Irreversible); typed-arg validation so LLM output never reaches SQL/shell (Principle IV).
- Bounded loop: max_iterations + max_tokens-per-run + wall-clock (config; defaults decided in plan); per-turn provider call via ai.Provider.Complete; tool-call→tool-result re-entry.
- Budget-cap run-start guard: refuse to start when month-to-date cost >= agent.monthly_budget_cents (status=failed, audited).
- Per-run audit via existing audit_entry (actor=agent principal, correlation_id threads the run).
- Token/cost capture via ai.Registry.Lookup(model).CostCents(usage).

Resolves deferred items:
- manyforge-deo §3.5: bind the agent principal's membership + a guard-safe role granting agents.run (NEW permission to seed) + tickets.{read,reply,write,assign}, so the agent passes RLS/RBAC to act.
- manyforge-1oq: agent-deletion FK semantics once an agent has acted (map 23503->409 / disable).
- manyforge-4po: AI Request temperature/sampling default.

US3 ships a degenerate fail-closed execution seam (Safe tools execute inline; non-Safe are proposed-only + audited, run goes awaiting_approval). US4 replaces that seam with the real autonomy gate + approval_item queue + three-mode matrix.

Regression contract (US3-owned pins): deterministic runs via ai.MockProvider/golden fixtures; every proposed+executed action audited; runs tenant-isolated (RLS, no-oracle 404); agent cannot invoke a tool outside allowed_tools; typed-arg validation pin; budget cap refuses start.

Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-03-us3-run-loop.md.

title: US2: agent definitions CRUD (business-bound agent table + AgentService over RLS)
description:
Spec 003 US2 (design §5). Add a business-bound 'agent' table and an AgentService doing CRUD over the RLS DB, with the agents.configure permission, ownership predicates, and no-oracle 404s.

Scope (design §3.2 columns): agent{ id, business_id, tenant_root_id, principal_id (→ kind='agent' principal), provider, model, system_prompt, allowed_tools text[], autonomy_mode smallint (1/2/3 default 1), enabled bool, monthly_budget_cents int, timestamps }. CreateAgent creates/links a kind='agent' principal scoped to the business. AgentService.{Create,Get,List,Update,Delete} run under db.WithPrincipal (RLS), dual-enforced ownership predicate (WHERE business scoping in SQL), foreign-UUID and unknown-UUID return the SAME not-found (no 403/404 oracle). New RLS table mirrors migration 0025 exactly (ENABLE not FORCE, authorized_businesses(current_principal()) policy, GRANT to manyforge_app, full reversible .down.sql) + db/schema.sql entry + db/query + sqlc generate. Add agents.configure to the RBAC permission catalog (migration after 0025) + role presets. Map errs via the credential.go mapErr idiom (pgx.ErrNoRows→ErrNotFound, 23505→ErrConflict).

Builds on US1a credential.go + migration 0025; mirrors internal/ticketing service/RLS patterns. Test plan: unit (validation) + integration (CRUD + seal-free round-trip + cross-tenant RLS no-oracle isolation via testdb/seedAgentTenant) + security_regression pins (tenant isolation, ownership predicate). HTTP/OpenAPI surface scope TBD at plan time (US1a creds were service-layer-only).

title: T039 send subscriber blocked: principal-less worker can't read/write RLS send tables
description:
The outbound send subscriber (internal/platform/notify/sender_subscriber.go) and the bounce path both call plain dbgen queries against RLS-protected tables (ticket_message, inbound_address) from the principal-less outbox worker (manyforge_app, NOBYPASSRLS, current_principal()=NULL).

Empirically (integration probe): authorized_businesses(NULL) returns 0 rows, so the ticket_message/inbound_address RLS USING predicate matches 0 rows. GetMessageDeliveryState/GetBusinessSystemInboundAddress return pgx.ErrNoRows and MarkMessageDelivered/MarkMessageFailed UPDATE 0 rows (silent no-op) when run via DB.WithTx.

Affected dbgen queries (all plain-table, all driven by the principal-less worker per their own doc comments): GetMessageDeliveryState, GetBusinessSystemInboundAddress, MarkMessageDelivered, MarkMessageFailed, GetOutboundMessageForBounce.

Fix (in-pattern, mirrors claim_outbox_batch / ingest_inbound_message in migrations 0014/0016): add a new migration (0019_*) with SECURITY DEFINER functions for the send/bounce delivery-state path, GRANT EXECUTE to manyforge_app, and repoint these db/query/ticketing.sql entries at them (RETURNS-TABLE DEFINERs via raw pgx per the plan's tech-stack note), then make generate. Out of scope for the T039 subscriber task (notify/main/config only); the plan's T035-prep landed these as plain-table queries and asserted 'no DEFINER change', which is the root oversight.

The subscriber + main.go wiring + config + DKIM are otherwise implemented and compile; only the DB layer below blocks the integration test (TestSendHappyPath/Suppressed/Idempotent).
close_reason: Fixed via migration 0019 SECURITY DEFINER (get_send_context + mark_message_delivery); subscriber repointed to raw-pgx DEFINER calls; send integration tests green.

title: US1 reply-token doesn't encode ticket's own id — p_hint_ticket threading fallback is dead
description:
In ingest_inbound_message (migrations/0014), the hint path resolves WHERE id = p_hint_ticket. But Go's inbox.Ingest mints reply_token over a FRESH uuid (not the ticket's row id, which the DB default generates), only to satisfy reply_token NOT NULL. So a later inbound reply carrying that token decodes to a uuid that never equals any ticket.id → the reply-token/VERP threading FALLBACK never matches. US1 threading is unaffected (it uses In-Reply-To/References header matching, which works + is tested). This breaks US2 outbound→inbound reply-token threading.

FIX (do in US2, TDD): write a failing test that (1) creates a ticket, (2) stamps Reply-To with SignReplyToken(ticket.id,key), (3) sends an inbound reply carrying that token with NO matching In-Reply-To header, (4) asserts it threads to the same ticket. It will fail. Then fix via the cleanest option: add p_ticket_id arg to ingest_inbound_message (new forward-only migration 0017, CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION) so Go generates the ticket uuid v7, signs the token over it, and passes both — making the stored ticket.reply_token coherent with the row id. (Alt: two-step UPDATE of reply_token after insert in the same tx.)

Minor related: attachment blob Key currently uses (tenantRoot, business, attachmentID, attachmentID) since ticket id isn't known pre-insert; functionally correct + tenant-scoped + unique, but a later task may want the real ticket id embedded (would require storing attachments after the ingest call). Flagged f2339ec.
notes:
RESOLVED. TDD surfaced TWO conspiring root causes, both fixed:
(1) id-coherence (documented): inbox.Ingest signed reply_token over uuid.New() (≠ ticket.id). Fix: migration 0017 adds p_ticket_id to ingest_inbound_message; Go now mints the ticket id (uuid v7), signs the token over it, and passes it as the new ticket's id.
(2) case-corruption (NEW, found via end-to-end RED): normalizeRecipient lowercased the WHOLE recipient incl. the case-sensitive base64url plus-token → VerifyReplyToken always failed. Fix: lowercase only the routing key (local+domain), preserve plus-token case verbatim.
Tests: internal/inbox/resolve_test.go (unit, case-preservation) + TestIngestRepliesThreadViaReplyToken (integration, end-to-end thread). Full gate green; migration 0017 up/down/up verified reversible.
close_reason: Reply-token/VERP threading fallback now works (two root causes fixed: id-coherence via migration 0017 p_ticket_id + plus-token case-preservation in normalizeRecipient). TDD red→green, full gate + migration reversibility verified.

title: Outbound dispatcher: reap stale in_progress ops (lease-based reclaim)
description: US4 T4 code-quality review (b7c69ed) found: claim_outbound_ops only selects WHERE status='pending'. An op goes pending→in_progress on claim. On the NORMAL failure path recordFailure→fail_outbound_op(terminal=false) requeues it to pending, so it is reclaimed. BUT if recordFailure's OWN tx fails (e.g. ctx cancelled on graceful shutdown so pool.Begin fails, or a transient DB blip exactly during the fail tx), the op is stranded in_progress with no path back — there is no reaper. Fix options: (a) widen claim_outbound_ops to also reclaim in_progress ops older than a lease window: WHERE status='pending' OR (status='in_progress' AND updated_at < now() - lease); or (b) add a requeue_stale_outbound_ops(interval) SECURITY DEFINER + a sweep tick in OutboundDispatcher.Run. Narrow double-fault window; deferred from T4 (scope: poller comment-path). Pin with a test that leaves an op in_progress and asserts it is reclaimed after the lease.

title: US3: agent deletion semantics once an agent has acted (FK RESTRICT on author_principal_id/notification)
description:
US2's DeleteAgent does a hard DELETE FROM principal (CTE) for the agent's kind='agent' principal. In US2 this is clean (no agent has acted). But FK review (US2 final review) found: ticket_message.author_principal_id (migration 0013) and notification.principal_id (0016) reference principal with NO ON DELETE action (RESTRICT). Once a US3+ agent authors a ticket_message (e.g. an approved reply) or receives a notification, DeleteAgent's principal delete will FK-violate (SQLSTATE 23503) — and mapAgentErr has no 23503 case, so it surfaces as a 500.

US3/US5 must decide agent-deletion semantics for agents that have acted: either (a) soft-delete/disable instead of hard delete (set enabled=false, keep the principal + audit trail), or (b) ON DELETE SET NULL on author_principal_id (keep the message, null the author), or (c) map 23503 → ErrConflict (409 'agent has activity, disable instead'). Option (a)+(c) preferred (preserves audit per Principle VI). Surfaced by US2 final review; not a US2 bug (no agent acts in US2).
close_reason: Fixed in 2438d3a: mapAgentErr maps 23503 FK-violation on agent delete to 409 (disable-instead); never-acted agents still cascade-delete cleanly. Pinned by agent_delete_fk_pin_test.go.

title: Connector clients discard the underlying transport error (log real cause before collapsing to ErrUnreachable)
description:
When httpClient.Do fails, jira/client.go:372 and zendesk/client.go:92 return fmt.Errorf("...: transport: %w", ErrUnreachable) and DISCARD the real underlying error entirely — it is not even logged server-side. A network failure, a TLS error, a DNS failure, a context timeout, and a netsafe SSRF dial-refusal all collapse into the identical opaque 'service unreachable'.

Impact: on 2026-06-12 a Little Snitch firewall block of the backend's outbound connections to Jira presented identically to a genuine upstream outage — every reconcile/outbound op logged only 'jira: transport: jira: service unreachable' with no cause. curl from the same host worked (curl had an allow rule; each Go binary is judged separately), but the logs gave zero signal pointing at the firewall. Cost a full multi-step investigation (probe binary replicating the netsafe dialer) to surface what a single log line would have shown.

Fix: before collapsing to the generic ErrUnreachable sentinel (which must STILL be what callers see — Principle II: never leak upstream/library internals to clients), log the real err server-side at WARN with enough context (connector_id, host, err). Mirrors the existing CLAUDE.md error-handling rule: 'Log the wrapped error server-side; return a stable error code + generic message.' Same treatment for the build-request branches (361 / 80) which also drop the cause. Consider including err.Error() only in the server log, never in the returned error.

Also see jira/client.go:394 (upstream status -> ErrUnreachable) which similarly hides the status code from logs.

Files: internal/connectors/jira/client.go, internal/connectors/zendesk/client.go. No behavior change for callers; logging-only + test that asserts the underlying cause is logged (e.g. a fake RoundTripper returning a sentinel error, assert it appears in a captured slog handler).
close_reason: jira+zendesk clients now log the real transport/build-request cause at WARN (method + query-stripped URL + err) before collapsing to ErrUnreachable; caller still sees only the sentinel (Principle II). In-package tests pin cause-logged + cause-not-leaked. test/sec-test/lint green.

title: US6 follow-up: MCP executor should split transport-error (reschedule) from result-isError (complete)
description: Task 9 review (US6): ApprovalExecutor.InvokeMCPTool currently maps an MCP tools/call result with isError=true to a Go error, which causes the approval to reschedule and re-dispatch -> double-fire of a foreign side effect that already happened on the server. A returned response (even isError) means the server processed the request; it should be treated as COMPLETED (MarkExecuted + feed the error result to the model, no reschedule). Only TRANSPORT errors (no response received) should reschedule. This is consistent with the at-least-once contract (design 3.6) today and only matters when the remote tool returns a business-level error after performing a side effect. Pair this with the terminal approval 'failed' state work in manyforge-sa8 so failure handling (transport vs result-error vs terminal-fail) is unified across internal and MCP tools.
close_reason: InvokeMCPTool now returns (out, toolErr, err): transport err reschedules; isError result completes (toolErr=true, content fed back), no reschedule/double-fire. Executor audits completed tool-error as executed:error. Host + executor unit tests pin both sides; old isError->error test inverted. test/sec-test/lint green.

title: US3: decide AI Request sampling-default handling (temperature unset vs explicit-0)
description:
US1b transports serialize Request.Temperature as-is. Because ai.Request.Temperature is a non-pointer float64, the transport cannot distinguish 'unset' from 'explicit 0', so an unset request sends temperature:0 (deterministic) to both Anthropic and OpenAI rather than the provider's natural default (~1.0). For a tool-calling agent runtime, deterministic-by-default is defensible, but it's currently implicit.

US3 decision when wiring agent definitions: either (a) make ai.Request.Temperature a *float64 (pointer; nil=provider default) — a US1a schema change rippling through both transports + mock, or (b) add ',omitempty' to the wire Temperature tags (unset 0 dropped → provider default; loses ability to force 0), or (c) keep current behavior and have agent definitions always set Temperature explicitly. Recommended: agent definition carries an explicit temperature (c), revisit pointer (a) only if model-default sampling is needed.

Surfaced by US1b final review. Files: internal/platform/ai/anthropic.go, openaicompat.go (Temperature wire fields), schema.go (Request.Temperature).

Repro: cache a token, restart backend (new keyring), reload SPA. Expected: a 401 on /me clears the session and routes to /login. Workaround today: click Sign out. Likely in the web/ auth interceptor/guard. Non-blocking; surfaced in the 002 HANDOFF across multiple sessions.
close_reason: NOT REPRODUCING. title: US4 follow-up: RSA-2048 DKIM fallback (Ed25519-only shipped)
description: T057 shipped Ed25519-only DKIM signing. research R3 specifies RSA-2048 as a fallback for receivers that don't validate Ed25519 DKIM. Add per-domain RSA-2048 keygen + signing as a fallback/secondary selector. Non-blocking; Ed25519 is widely supported and go-msgauth signs+verifies it.
design:
SCOPED 2026-06-02 (deferred to feature work, NOT polish — ~MEDIUM, multi-day).

GOAL: 'RSA-2048 fallback for receivers that don't validate Ed25519 DKIM' (research R3). The ONLY design that achieves this is DUAL-PUBLISH + DUAL-SIGN (not pick-one-per-domain): generate BOTH keys, publish TWO DNS TXT records (k=ed25519 + k=rsa), emit TWO DKIM-Signature headers so each receiver validates whichever it supports.

CHANGE MAP (file:line):
1. Keygen — internal/ticketing/identity.go:~190 CreateEmailDomain() ed25519.GenerateKey only → also rsa.GenerateKey(2048); seal both.
2. Schema — migrations/ NEW migration: add dkim_rsa_selector, dkim_rsa_public_key, dkim_rsa_private_key_ref to email_domain (+ db/schema.sql + make generate for dbgen). get_send_identity DEFINER (migration 0023) must also return the RSA columns.
3. Send path — internal/platform/notify/sender_subscriber.go:189-228 selectCustomIdentity(): line 216 len(keyBytes)!=ed25519.PrivateKeySize HARD-REJECTS non-ed25519 — must load+unseal BOTH keys; smtp.go signDKIM already crypto.Signer-agnostic (no change) but Send must sign twice (two DKIM-Signature headers) OR Mail carries []*DKIMConfig.
4. DNS publication — internal/ticketing/identity.go:~522 toEmailDomain() hardcodes 'k=ed25519' → expose a SECOND DKIMRecord for the RSA selector. API contract change.
5. OpenAPI — contracts/openapi.yaml DNSChallenge: dkim_record (single) → dkim_records (list) or add dkim_rsa_record.
6. Tests — internal/platform/notify/dkim_signing_integration_test.go (DB+infra): add RSA keygen+sign+verify; assert BOTH signatures present + cryptographically valid. Plus identity keygen test.

NOTE: system DKIM (cmd/manyforge/main.go dkimConfigFromCfg) ALREADY parses both PKCS8-ed25519 and PKCS1-RSA — custom keygen should mirror. Ed25519 is widely supported so this is genuinely non-blocking.
notes: Deferred to feature work (decision 2026-06-02). Backlog-clear pass closed the other 6 spec-002 follow-ups (9li/0yv/zzr/no9/7c0/mu7); 3jt is feature-scale (dual-publish DKIM), see design field.

title: Authenticated JSON API lacks request-body-size cap (httpx.DecodeJSON)
description: Final US3 review (T044-T051) flagged: httpx.DecodeJSON streams the request body with no http.MaxBytesReader, and the /api/v1 protected routes have no global request-size middleware (only ReadHeaderTimeout is set; InboundMaxBytes covers only webhook/SMTP). description: Code-quality nits from US7 reviews: (1) run_cursor.go could adopt the parameterized encodeCursor(kind,keyset) core like internal/ticketing/cursor.go before a 2nd agents list endpoint; the page-1 sentinel (9999-01-01 + all-Fs UUID) are magic values worth naming. (2) clampRunLimit lacks the DoS-cap doc comment that ticketing.clampLimit has. (3) OpenAPI 'limit' param should document default(50)/max(100).

title: US7 follow-up: date-only custom window midnight truncation + budget_pct UI hint
description: Design §6 known: a custom window to=YYYY-MM-DD parses to UTC midnight and the half-open [from,to) excludes that whole day. The API/OpenAPI accept 'custom' even though the UI never sends it, so an API caller can hit this. Options: normalize 'to' to end-of-day, or document clearly. Also surface a 'budget % shown only for this month' hint in the accounting UI. From US7 final review.

title: US7 follow-up: remove redundant dead ListAgentRunsByAgent query
description: db/query/agent_run.sql has an older unbounded ListAgentRunsByAgent (LIMIT $3) that predates US7 (US3 commit 26abcc2) and is never called from hand-written code. US7's keyset ListAgentRuns supersedes it. Remove + regenerate sqlc. Pre-existing, not a US7 regression.

title: US7 follow-up: test LoadModelRegistry empty-catalog fail-loud path
description: model_pricing_test.go covers the WithTx-error path but not the reg.Len()==0 -> error branch (the 'fail loudly on unseeded catalog' invariant). Add a unit test where WithTx succeeds but returns zero rows. From US7 final review.

title: US6 follow-up: legacy SSE transport fallback for MCP
description: v1 targets Streamable-HTTP (2025-06-18). Some MCP servers may only speak the legacy HTTP+SSE transport. Add a fallback path if a target server requires it. Spec 003 US6 §6.

title: US6 follow-up: MCP OAuth / richer auth schemes
description: v1 sealed_auth_ref supports only {scheme:bearer,token}. Add OAuth (client-credentials / authorization-code) and other auth schemes for MCP servers. Extend the sealed blob shape + connect flow. Spec 003 US6 §6.

title: US6 follow-up: tools/list caching across runs
description: v1 connects to each opted-in MCP server and tools/list's at every run start (per-run connection lifecycle). Cache discovered tool defs per server with a TTL/invalidation to cut per-run latency + load on remote servers. Perf optimization. Spec 003 US6 §5.

title: US3 nit: validate agent_run target_type + document target_id as hint-not-gate
description: Holistic review (US3 close-out): agent_run.target_type is free-text (no CHECK) while trigger has a CHECK — add a CHECK or service-layer allowlist {'ticket'} for consistency (blast radius is nil today: parameterized SQL + string-compare only). Also add a one-line comment at RunService.Trigger noting target_id is a prompt HINT, not an ownership gate (tools are the authorization boundary under the agent's RLS) so a future reader doesn't mistake it for a missed ownership check.

title: US3 follow-up: share permission-key constants between tool registry, ticketing handlers, and migrations
description: internal/agents/tools.go hardcodes RequiredPerm string literals (tickets.read/write/assign/reply); internal/ticketing/handler.go and the 0015 migration use the same bare strings. If the permission catalog ever defines exported Perm* constants, these call sites should reference them so a rename fails at compile time rather than silently mis-gating. Low risk (perms are migration-seeded). Surfaced in US3 Task 4 code-quality review (M2).

title: knownProviders set should track the ai_provider PG enum
description: internal/agents/credential.go knownProviders is an untyped map[string]bool that must stay in lockstep with the ai_provider Postgres enum (migration 0025) + dbgen.AiProvider* constants. Convert to dbgen.AiProvider* constants or a generated set when the enum next changes (e.g. adding a provider). Flagged in US1a final review; non-blocking. Part of Spec 003 (manyforge-deo).

title: US4 follow-up: DKIM master-key base64/hex parse disambiguation
description: internal/platform/config envKey32 prefers the decode yielding exactly 32 bytes; a 64-char all-hex-alphabet base64 string is taken as a 32-byte hex key. Cannot arise from a correctly-encoded 32-byte key (44-char base64 or 64-char hex are unambiguous). Optional hardening: require explicit base64:/hex: prefix or anchor hex on ^[0-9a-fA-F]{64}$. Nit.
